content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 17, 2011

Anthony Lewis
General Counsel
California Technology Agency
1325 J Street, Suite 1600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-11-080

Dear Mr. Lewis:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your inquiry is general in nature and does not involve specific governmental decisions we are treating it as a request for informal advice.²

You are general counsel with the California Technology Agency. You are writing to request formal written advice on behalf of Ryan Dulin, a CEA II (working title Chief, California 9-1-1 Emergency Communications Division). Mr. Dulin has submitted a Form 700 which identifies investments (stock ownership) in two businesses Motorola and Oracle that conduct business with the California Technology Agency. As chief of the 9-1-1 Emergency Communications Division, Mr. Dulin can be required to manage the program budget and provide vendor oversight to reduce costs. Mr. Dulin has not directly engaged in any contractual or financial transactions involving these two companies identified in his Form 700.

You wish to know whether Mr. Dulin's investments in the above named companies would pose a conflict-of-interest with his duties as chief of the 9-1-1 Emergency Communications Division. Please note that determining whether a conflict-of-interest exists in a given situation is a fact-sensitive analysis.

_______________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed).

File No. I-11-057
Page No. 2

Because your inquiry is general in nature and you do not provide us with any specific
governmental decisions, we have enclosed the following advice letters for your review: Reiter
Advice Letter, I-06-113; Larson Advice Letter, I-06-073; and Reyes Advice Letter, A-04-210.
We hope that these letters will be helpful in providing general guidance with regard to
Mr. Dulin's situation.

In the future, should you have questions regarding a specific governmental decision,
please contact us for further advice. Or, in the alternative we can provide formal written advice
to the public officials in question.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

ER:jgl

Enclosures

sections.question: Whether Mr. Dulin's investments in Motorola and Oracle companies would pose a conflict-of-interest with his duties as chief of the 9-1-1 Emergency Communications Division, given that these companies conduct business with the California Technology Agency and he can be required to manage the program budget and provide vendor oversight.
sections.conclusion: Because your inquiry is general in nature and you do not provide us with any specific governmental decisions, we have enclosed the following advice letters for your review: Reiter Advice Letter, 1-06-113; Larson Advice Letter, 1-06-073; and Reyes Advice Letter, A-04-210. We hope that these letters will be helpful in providing general guidance with regard to Mr. Dulin's situation. In the future, should you have questions regarding a specific governmental decision, please contact us for further advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a California Technology Agency official's stock investments in two companies that conduct business with the agency create a conflict of interest given his responsibilities for budget management and vendor oversight.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide specific advice on the general inquiry and instead directed the requestor to review three previously issued advice letters for general guidance. The FPPC invited the requestor to resubmit with specific governmental decisions for formal written advice.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice letter. No formally numbered QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections found; question and conclusion extracted from narrative text. File number contains discrepancy (header shows 1-11-080, page 2 shows I-11-057). Letter emphasizes that specific governmental decisions are needed for formal advice.

QUESTION: Whether Mr. Dulin's investments in Motorola and Oracle companies would pose a conflict-of-interest with his duties as chief of the 9-1-1 Emergency Communications Division, given that these companies conduct business with the California Technology Agency and he can be required to manage the program budget and provide vendor oversight.

CONCLUSION: Because your inquiry is general in nature and you do not provide us with any specific governmental decisions, we have enclosed the following advice letters for your review: Reiter Advice Letter, 1-06-113; Larson Advice Letter, 1-06-073; and Reyes Advice Letter, A-04-210. We hope that these letters will be helpful in providing general guidance with regard to Mr. Dulin's situation. In the future, should you have questions regarding a specific governmental decision, please contact us for further advice.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to a general inquiry regarding whether a California Technology Agency official's stock investments in Motorola and Oracle create a conflict of interest, declining to provide specific guidance and instead referring the requestor to three analogous advice letters.
